document.hierarchy_note: Single sterling revolving credit facility agreement between one lender and one borrower; not a multi-facility deal document or individual loan instrument.

source_refs.quote: CONFORMED COPY Facility Agreement between The Secretary of State for Transport as Lender and Network Rail Infrastructure Limited as Borrower Dated 4 July 2014

borrowers.name: Network Rail Infrastructure Limited
borrowers.registered_office: Kings Place, 90 York Way, London N1 9AG

source_refs.quote: NETWORK RAIL INFRASTRUCTURE LIMITED, (the "Borrower"), registered in England and Wales as company number 02904587 and having its registered office at Kings Place, 90 York Way, London N1 9AG.

lenders.name: The Secretary of State for Transport
lenders.legal_name: THE SECRETARY OF STATE FOR TRANSPORT
lenders.address: Department for Transport, Great Minster House, 33 Horseferry Road, London SW1P 4DR

source_refs.quote: THE SECRETARY OF STATE FOR TRANSPORT, (the "Lender")

source_refs.title: Notice Details
source_refs.quote: THE SECRETARY OF STATE FOR TRANSPORT Address: Department for Transport Great Minster House 33 Horseferry Road London SW1P 4DR

other_parties.name: Department for Transport
other_parties.role: Notice addressee for Lender / government department address
other_parties.address: Great Minster House, 33 Horseferry Road, London SW1P 4DR

source_refs.title: Notice Details
source_refs.quote: Address: Department for Transport Great Minster House 33 Horseferry Road London SW1P 4DR

facility.purpose: Loans may be utilised solely for financing Permitted Business, including repaying one or more intercompany loans from NRIF or funding amounts payable under the Finance Documents (including interest, default interest, drawdown fee, costs and indemnities, but excluding principal prepayments under Clause 4.2).
purpose_restrictions: Excludes de minimis business conducted in accordance with conditions 4.7 to 4.10 of the Network Licence and any business or activity consented to by ORR under Condition 4 of the Network Licence.
Excludes refinancing of Financial Indebtedness originally incurred for such excluded business or activity as refinanced from time to time.
The Lender is not bound to monitor or verify application of Loans.

source_refs.title: The Facility
source_refs.quote: the Lender makes available to the Borrower a sterling revolving credit facility in an aggregate amount equal to the Commitment.

legal.jurisdiction_clause: The parties irrevocably submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England for disputes connected with the agreement and related non-contractual obligations.

source_refs.title: Governing Law
source_refs.quote: governed by, and interpreted in accordance with, the laws of England

source_refs.title: Third Party Rights
source_refs.quote: No person who is not a party to this Agreement will have any rights under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999

interest_terms.reference_rate_source: UK Debt Management Office national loan funds new loans rate under heading 'maturity', determined using HM Treasury methodology under section 5 of the National Loans Act 1968, published on the DMO website at 9.30 a.m. on the relevant Utilisation Date for loans of the same maturity after deduction of the DMO margin (0.11% as at agreement date), or such other rate as agreed.

capitalisation.compounding_method: Default interest, if unpaid, is compounded with the overdue amount at the end of each Interest Period applicable to that overdue amount while remaining immediately due and payable.

source_refs.quote: 1.50 per. cent per annum

source_refs.reference: Definition of National Loans Fund Interest Rate

source_refs.title: Day Count Convention
source_refs.quote: calculated on the basis of the actual number of days elapsed and a year of 365 days.

availability_and_utilisation.availability_basis: Availability Period from and including the agreement date to 31 March 2019. Utilisation Request must be delivered by 11:00 a.m. five Business Days before proposed Utilisation Date for Loans not exceeding GBP 500,000,000 and ten Business Days before for Loans equal to or exceeding GBP 500,000,000, unless otherwise agreed. Proposed Utilisation Date must be a Business Day within the Availability Period.

source_refs.quote: and ending on 31 March 2019

source_refs.title: Utilisation
source_refs.quote: Only one Loan may be requested in each Utilisation Request. The aggregate amount of Loans drawn on the same day must not be more than £2,000,000,000

instalments.date_description: Each Loan must be repaid in full on the Repayment Date stated in the relevant Utilisation Request and in any event on or prior to the Termination Date.
instalments.description: Bullet repayment per Loan on its stated Repayment Date, with final backstop no later than 1 April 2029.

source_refs.quote: 1 April 2029 or such other date agreed by the parties

payments_and_settlement.business_day_definition: A day other than a Saturday or Sunday on which banks are open for general business in London.

payment_instructions.payer: The Secretary of State for Transport
payment_instructions.description: Lender must make required payments available to the Borrower for value on the due date at such time and in sterling and to the relevant account designated by the Borrower.

payment_instructions.payee: The Secretary of State for Transport
payment_instructions.description: Borrower must make required payments to the Lender in same day funds to the account or bank specified by the Lender.

payments_and_settlement.set_off_terms: Borrower payments must be calculated without reference to any set-off or counterclaim and made free and clear of any deduction for set-off or counterclaim; the Lender may set off matured obligations due from or owed by the Borrower under the agreement against matured obligations owed by the Lender to the Borrower other than grants.

source_refs.quote: banks are open for general business in London

source_refs.title: Pari passu ranking
source_refs.quote: rank at least pari passu with the claims of a ll its other unsecured and unsubordinated creditors

items.description: Certified copy of the constitutional documents of the Borrower.

Certified copy of Borrower board resolutions approving the facility terms and execution of required documents, authorising specified persons to execute the documents and to take actions required to implement and operate the Facility.
Specimen signatures of each person authorised by the board resolution.
Legal opinion of Clifford Chance LLP, legal advisers to the Borrower, as to due incorporation and due authorisation of, and execution by, the Borrower.

Forecast for the period to 31 March 2015 of aggregate utilisations in each calendar month and aggregate Loans at month end, and for the period from 1 April 2015 to expiry of the Availability Period of aggregate utilisations in each Financial Year and aggregate Loans at each Financial Year end.

Drawdown conditions on date of Utilisation Request and proposed Utilisation Date: for Rollover Loans no Event of Default continuing or resulting; for other Loans no Default continuing or resulting; Repeating Representations true in all material respects; non-Rollover Loan financial indebtedness limits under Network Licence conditions 3.1 and 3.2 not exceeded; and Financial Year drawing caps tied to Contingencies Fund/Supply and Appropriation approvals unless the Lender nevertheless agrees.
items.qualifier: Conditions in Clause 2.2 apply on both the date of the Utilisation Request and the proposed Utilisation Date.

representations.materiality_qualifier: Repeating Representations are drawdown conditions only if true in all material respects; certain representation/EoD consequences include Material Adverse Effect and knowledge/belief qualifiers.

items.description: Borrower is a limited liability company duly incorporated and validly existing under the laws of England, with power to own its assets and carry on its business.
Borrower has power to enter into and perform the agreement and has taken necessary action to authorise entry into and performance of the agreement and transactions contemplated by it.
Subject to Legal Reservations, Borrower obligations under the agreement are legal, binding, valid and enforceable.
Entry into and performance of the agreement and contemplated transactions do not conflict with laws/regulations applicable to the Borrower or its constitutional documents.

Required authorisations, licences, consents and approvals in connection with entry into, performance, validity and enforceability of the agreement and contemplated transactions have been or will be obtained/effected by the date required and, to the extent obtained/effected, are in full force and effect.

No litigation, arbitration or administrative proceeding which, if adversely determined, might reasonably be expected to have a Material Adverse Effect, has to the best of Borrower's knowledge and belief been started or threatened against it.
items.qualifier: Knowledge and belief; Material Adverse Effect.

items.description: As at agreement date and first Utilisation Date only, no Event of Default has occurred and is continuing or might reasonably be expected to result from any Utilisation.
items.qualifier: Limited to agreement date and first Utilisation Date only.

items.description: Borrower's payment obligations under the Finance Documents rank at least pari passu with all other unsecured and unsubordinated creditors, except obligations mandatorily preferred by law applying to companies generally.

financial_covenants.description: Restrictions on Financial Indebtedness by the Borrower, Borrower Group and Holding Companies for financing or in connection with Permitted Business, subject to enumerated exceptions including this Agreement, Intra-Group Loans, ordinary-course FX/commodities hedging, pre-disclosed ordinary-course counter indemnities/performance bonds/parent guarantees, permitted renewals/amendments, Existing Hedging unwind swaps and overdraft/other borrowing facilities up to GBP 50,000,000 aggregate.
financial_covenants.threshold: GBP 50,000,000 aggregate limit for overdraft facility and other borrowing facilities exception; other exceptions as specified in Clause 9.2(a).

financial_covenants.description: Restrictions on Financial Indebtedness benefiting from or supported by the Financial Indemnity, subject to exceptions for certain pre-disclosed ordinary-course counter indemnities for letters of credit, permitted renewals/amendments, Existing Hedging unwind swaps and overdraft/other borrowing facilities up to GBP 50,000,000 aggregate.
financial_covenants.threshold: GBP 50,000,000 aggregate limit for overdraft facility and other borrowing facilities exception; other exceptions as specified in Clause 9.2(b).

information_undertakings: On at least 14 days' notice, provide all information, including forecasts and estimates of expenditure and services, reasonably required to ensure submissions to Parliament and HM Treasury for Estimates and any Vote on Account are consistent with parliamentary and HM Treasury requirements.

On or before the 6th Business Day of each calendar month, provide a forecast of expected draw and repayment amounts for the following calendar month, with changes not exceeding GBP 10,000,000 permitted on or before the date falling 6 Business Days after the Delivery Date.

On 30 September 2014 and each 31 March and 30 September thereafter, provide updated 12-month monthly utilisation/loan forecasts; and on each 31 March provide Financial Year forecasts through expiry of the Availability Period, or other forecasts as agreed.
general_undertakings: Borrower must not transfer, dispose of, create or allow Security over Station Access Charges, certain future Regulated Contract revenues, Track Access Charges, or grants by the Lender or Scottish Ministers to the Borrower.
Financial indebtedness restrictions in Clause 9.2 do not apply where the Lender has repudiated, or is in breach of, its obligation to lend under the agreement.

Clause 9.2 does not prevent Financial Indebtedness that does not benefit from or receive support from the Financial Indemnity for business/activity falling within Clause 2.3(A)(1), provided it is disclosed to the Lender in writing before the agreement date or before incurrence.
covenants_and_undertakings.debt_restrictions: Borrower, Borrower Group and Holding Companies are restricted from incurring Financial Indebtedness for Permitted Business or supported by the Financial Indemnity, except for detailed permitted categories; overdraft and other borrowing facilities are limited to GBP 50,000,000 aggregate at any time.

events.description: Borrower fails to pay any sum due within five Business Days of being informed in writing by the Lender that the sum has become due, other than as a direct result of the Lender not complying with an obligation to fund.
events.heading: Termination
events.remedy_or_consequence: If continuing, Lender may accelerate all or part of outstanding Loans, accrued interest and other amounts, make them payable on demand and/or cancel the Commitment.

events.description: Borrower ceases to hold the Network Licence.
events.heading: Termination

events.description: Representation or statement in Finance Documents or related documents is or proves incorrect or misleading in any material respect when made or deemed made and, if capable of remedy, is not remedied within 60 days after notice or Borrower awareness.
events.heading: Termination

events.description: Borrower fails to comply with any Finance Document provision other than payment obligations and, if capable of remedy, does not remedy within 60 days after notice or Borrower awareness.
events.heading: Termination

events.description: Borrower, except for Lender-approved solvent corporate restructuring, goes into liquidation or administration, is dissolved, has a liquidator/receiver/administrator/official/creditors representative appointed over itself or a substantial part of assets, becomes insolvent, or ceases/threatens to cease all or a substantial part of business.
events.heading: Termination

events.description: Insolvency or similar proceedings are initiated and not, in Lender's opinion, disputed in good faith; encumbrancer takes possession of substantially all assets; distress/execution/diligence/process is levied/enforced/sued against substantially all assets and not discharged or ceased within 14 days; or Borrower initiates/consents to insolvency proceedings or makes conveyance/assignment for benefit of creditors generally.
events.heading: Termination

events.description: It is or becomes unlawful for the Borrower to perform any obligations under the Finance Documents.
events.heading: Termination

events.description: An event of default, however described and excluding an FI Provider Event of Default, has occurred and is continuing in relation to Financial Indebtedness benefiting from or supported by the Financial Indemnity.
events.heading: Termination

events.description: Borrower sells, leases, transfers or otherwise disposes of all or substantially all of its assets in a single or series of related or unrelated, voluntary or involuntary transactions.
events.heading: Termination

events.description: A person obtains Control of the Borrower, the Lender has not approved the obtaining of Control for agreement purposes, the Lender serves notice within one month stating it proposes to exercise rights unless the person ceases to have Control within three months, and that cessation does not occur within that period.
events.heading: Termination

source_refs.title: Termination
source_refs.quote: Each of the following events or circumstances is an Event of Default

events_of_default.acceleration_and_enforcement_rights: If an Event of Default has occurred and is continuing, the Lender may by notice declare all or part of any outstanding Loan, accrued interest and other amounts payable, including under Clause 5.5, immediately due and payable and/or payable on demand, and/or cancel the Commitment.

mandatory_prepayment_triggers.description: Amounts may become immediately due and payable or payable on demand after a continuing Event of Default if declared by the Lender under Clause 10.2.
Change of Control is an Event of Default only if the Clause 10.1(J) approval, notice and three-month cure/cessation mechanics are satisfied.

cancellation_events.description: On a continuing Event of Default, Lender may cancel the Commitment by notice.
Available Commitment unutilised at that time is immediately cancelled at the end of the Availability Period.

prepayment_and_cancellation.change_of_control: A Change of Control event requires unapproved Control, Lender notice within one month proposing exercise of Clause 10.2 rights unless Control ceases within three months, and failure of that cessation within the three-month period.

source_refs.title: Voluntary Cancellation
source_refs.quote: The Borrower may, if it gives the Lender five Business Days’ notice, cancel the whole or any part of the Available Commitment.

fees.payable_to: The Secretary of State for Transport
fees.description: Drawdown fee of 0.10% of the amount of each Loan payable in accordance with the Lender's payment terms.

fees.payable_to: The Secretary of State for Transport
fees.description: Borrower must reimburse Lender, within five Business Days of demand, for reasonable costs and expenses including legal fees incurred responding to, evaluating, negotiating or complying with Borrower amendment, waiver or consent requests.

fees.payable_to: The Secretary of State for Transport
fees.description: Borrower must pay, within five Business Days of demand, Lender costs and expenses including legal fees incurred in connection with enforcement or preservation of rights and related proceedings.

fees_and_expenses.lender_preparation_costs: Indemnities cover costs/losses/liabilities reasonably incurred due to Events of Default, payment failure, investigations reasonably believed to be Events of Default, failed funding of requested Loans other than due to Lender default/negligence alone, failure to prepay according to notice, and HM Treasury Penalty Charges caused by mismatch with forecasts under Clause 9.4.

source_refs.title: Drawdown Fee
source_refs.quote: The Borrower must pay to the Lender a drawdown fee of 0.10 per cent. of the amount of each Loan

public_sector_terms.lender_statutory_authority: The Lender enters into the agreement in exercise of its power in section 6 of the Railways Act 2005 and under the authority given by section 55 of that Act.
public_sector_terms.permitted_business_constraints: Loans may only finance Permitted Business as defined by the Network Licence, subject to express exclusions for certain de minimis/ORR-consented business and refinancing of indebtedness incurred for excluded business.
public_sector_terms.public_policy_constraints: Drawdowns are constrained by parliamentary and HM Treasury supply/estimate processes: before Royal Assent to the Supply and Appropriation (Main Estimates) Bill for FY ending 31 March 2015, capped at GBP 550,000,000 from the Contingencies Fund; thereafter capped by amounts approved in Supply and Appropriation Acts for advances under the Facility unless the Lender agrees. Borrower must supply forecasts/information for Estimates and Votes on Account.

source_refs.quote: The Lender enters into this Agreement in exercise of its power in section 6 of the Railways Act 2005 and under the authority given by section 55 of that Act.
information submissions to parliament and HM Treasury for the purposes of Estimates and any Vote on Account

tax_and_withholding.withholding_tax_provisions: All payments by the Borrower to the Lender must be made free and clear of and without deduction for or on account of Tax unless the Borrower is required by law to make the payment subject to deduction or withholding of Tax. No express tax gross-up is extracted from the clause.

regulator_consents_required: ORR approval is relevant to increases in Value of the RAB reflecting additional projects contemplated in the CP5 Regulatory Settlement for a possible commitment increase under Clause 2.4(B)(2).
Network Licence financial indebtedness limits in conditions 3.1 and 3.2 must not be exceeded for non-Rollover Loans as a drawdown condition, unless the Lender nevertheless agrees.
licence_or_permit_conditions: Borrower must hold the Network Licence; ceasing to hold it is an Event of Default.
Permitted Business is defined by reference to the Network Licence.

source_refs.reference: Definitions of CP5 Regulatory Settlement, Network Licence and ORR

source_refs.quote: the Borrower ceases to hold the Network Licence

reporting_and_information.financial_statement_delivery: No financial-statement delivery covenant extracted; reporting focuses on forecasts and information for parliamentary/HM Treasury Estimates and Vote on Account purposes.
reporting_and_information.budget_or_business_plan_delivery: Forecasts required monthly and semiannually/annually regarding expected drawdowns, repayments and aggregate Loans over specified periods through expiry of the Availability Period.
event_notice_requirements: Borrower may submit forecast changes not exceeding GBP 10,000,000 on or before the date falling 6 Business Days after the monthly Delivery Date.

Borrower must disclose Financial Indebtedness for Clause 2.3(A)(1) excluded business/activity that does not benefit from or receive support from the Financial Indemnity before agreement date or before incurrence.

notice_addresses.party_name: The Secretary of State for Transport
notice_addresses.address: Department for Transport, Great Minster House, 33 Horseferry Road, London SW1P 4DR
Kings Place, 90 York Way, London, N1 9AG

deemed_delivery_rules: Notices by post deemed served on the fifth Business Day following posting.
Notices delivered by hand or sent by email or facsimile deemed served when received in legible form.
Communications/documents becoming effective after 5.00 p.m. in the place of receipt are deemed effective on the following Business Day.

notice_periods.period: not less than five Business Days' notice
not less than five Business Days' notice

notice_mechanics.change_of_details_mechanics: Either party may notify a substitute address, fax number, telephone number, department or officer by not less than five Business Days' notice.

source_refs.title: Notice Details
source_refs.quote: SCHEDULE 1: NOTICE DETAILS

extraction_notes: Full text extracted with extract_pdf_text to data/04_facility_uk_gov_dft_facility_agreement.json.source.txt before JSON extraction.
Document is a conformed copy dated 4 July 2014 for a single sterling revolving credit facility from a UK public-sector lender to Network Rail Infrastructure Limited.
No separate agent, arranger, guarantor or security trustee is named in the extracted text.

No standalone security documents or guarantees are described; unsecured status is supported by pari passu unsecured ranking, while Clause 9.1 contains a negative pledge/restricted security undertaking over specified revenues/grants.
Email addresses and fax numbers in Schedule 1 appear blank/redacted in extracted text and are not populated.
known_omissions: Not all boilerplate definitions are separately normalised beyond material facility economics, public-sector constraints, covenants, events of default and operational terms.
Signature names are not visible in the extracted text; execution block only shows seal/signature placeholders.
